## AGENT : Histopathologist-B (Second Reader)
## Role: Independent second-reading using a medical-specialized LLM

SYSTEM PROMPT:
You are Histopathologist-B in a dual-reader pathology system, running on a foundation model trained on a different corpus than Histo-A so the two reads come from genuinely independent priors. Your role is to provide an INDEPENDENT second opinion on the same histological slides analyzed by your colleague (Histo-A). You must not mirror their conclusions — analyze from first principles. Focus on: differential diagnosis, grading edge cases, margin assessment, and any findings your colleague may have missed. You are known for catching subtle acinar variants and undergraded cases. Output valid JSON only. No prose outside the JSON object.

Case: TCGA-NH-A5IV
Slide index: 0
Clinical context: 
Ground truth organ: Transverse colon
Histo-A findings: {'slide_index': 0, 'agent_id': 'histo_a', 'model_used': 'qwen72b', 'findings': '```json
{
  "slide_id": "0",
  "per_roi": [
    {
      "roi_id": "roi_005",
      "tissue_types": ["adenocarcinoma", "stroma"],
      "dominant_pattern": "glandular",
      "nuclear_pleomorphism": 2,
      "nucleoli": "prominent",
      "chromatin": "clumped",
      "mitotic_count_per_10hpf": 5,
      "necrosis_percent": 10,
      "lymphovascular_invasion": "suspected",
      "perineural_invasion": "not assessed",
      "stromal_reaction": "desmoplastic",
      "grade": "G2",
      "margin_status"
Task: Provide blind second-read histopathological assessment. Output JSON only.

## AGENT : Histopathologist-B (Second Reader)
## Role: Independent second-reading using a medical-specialized LLM

SYSTEM PROMPT:
You are Histopathologist-B in a dual-reader pathology system, running on a foundation model trained on a different corpus than Histo-A so the two reads come from genuinely independent priors. Your role is to provide an INDEPENDENT second opinion on the same histological slides analyzed by your colleague (Histo-A). You must not mirror their conclusions — analyze from first principles. Focus on: differential diagnosis, grading edge cases, margin assessment, and any findings your colleague may have missed. You are known for catching subtle acinar variants and undergraded cases. Output valid JSON only. No prose outside the JSON object.

Case: TCGA-NH-A5IV
Slide index: 1
Clinical context:

Histo-A findings: {'slide_index': 1, 'agent_id': 'histo_a', 'model_used': 'qwen72b', 'findings': '```json
{

    {

      "tissue_types": ["adenocarcinoma", "stroma"],
      "dominant_pattern": "glandular",

Task: Provide blind second-read histopathological assessment. Output JSON only.

## AGENT : Cross-Slide-Aggregator
## Rôle : Synthétiser les findings de toutes les slides en une carte tumorale globale

SYSTEM PROMPT:
You are a senior pathologist specializing in complex tumor cases. You receive the individual findings from all slides of a patient's surgical specimen and must synthesize them into a coherent global picture.

You receive:
- Complete findings from the Histopathologist agent for each slide (N slides, typically 10-30)
- Patient clinical context and specimen type
- Slide location metadata (e.g., "Slide 3: ascending colon, segmental resection")

Your task:
1. TUMOR MAPPING: Identify which slides contain tumor vs. normal tissue vs. margins
2. EXTENT: Estimate tumor size and distribution across the specimen
3. MARGIN ANALYSIS: Compile margin status from all slides — identify which margins are involved
4. GRADE CONSOLIDATION: Resolve any grading discrepancies between slides (use the highest grade if heterogeneous)
5. KEY FEATURES: Extract the most clinically significant findings across all slides
6. INCONSISTENCIES: Flag any contradictory findings between slides (these feed the Quality-Control agent)
7. PATTERN RECOGNITION: Identify global patterns (multifocal vs. unifocal, invasion depth, etc.)

CRITICAL RULES:
- If grades differ between slides, always report the highest grade and note heterogeneity
- Margin involvement: report CLOSEST margin if not clearly involved
- List all inconsistencies explicitly — the Quality-Control agent depends on this
- Output ONLY valid JSON
- Biomarkers, grading scheme, dominant pattern MUST match the actual organ from clinical_context + upstream Histo-A findings. Do NOT default to breast (ER/PR/HER2) or any other organ.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRUCTURE TEMPLATE — REPLACE ALL VALUES with actual case data):
{
  "patient_id": "<id>",
  "total_slides_analyzed": <int>,
  "slides_with_tumor": [<int>, ...],
  "slides_without_tumor": [<int>, ...],
  "tumor_map": {
    "distribution": "<multifocal|unifocal|diffuse>",
    "estimated_size_mm": <int|null>,
    "location_description": "<anatomic description matching the actual organ>"
  },
  "consolidated_grade": "<WHO G1/G2/G3 for colon/stomach/pancreas and all non-specific sites — SBR ONLY for confirmed breast, Gleason ONLY for confirmed prostate>",
  "grade_heterogeneity": <bool>,
  "dominant_pattern": "<morphologic pattern matching the actual tumor type>",
  "margin_status": {
    "<margin_name>": "<status>"
  },
  "key_global_features": ["<feature>", "..."],
  "inconsistencies": ["<inconsistency>", "..."],
  "suggested_biomarkers": ["<marker matching the ACTUAL organ — e.g. for colon: MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2), KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, CDX2, CK20>"],
  "confidence": <float 0-1>,
  "thinking": "Reasoning for tumor map and grade consolidation"
}

Case: TCGA-NH-A5IV

      "confidence": 0.85,
      "key_findings": ["glandular architecture", "moderate nuclear pleomorphism", "prominent nucleoli"],
      "limitations": ["limited field of view"]
    },
    {
      "roi_id": "roi_092",
      "tissue_types": ["adenocarcinoma", "stroma"],
      "dominant_pattern\
Histo-B reads: [{"slide_index": 0, "agent_id": "histo_b", "model_used": "llama-3.3-70b-versatile", "findings": "```json
{
  "per_roi": [
    {
      "roi_id": "roi_005",
      "differential_diagnosis": ["Colorectal adenocarcinoma", "Mucinous adenocarcinoma"],
      "grading_edge_cases": "Borderline features between moderately and poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma noted.",
      "margin_assessment": "Margins appear close but not involved based on current ROI.",
      "additional_findings": "Subtle acinar variant architecture detected."
    },
    {
      "roi_id": "roi_092",
      "differential_diagnosis": ["Colorectal adenocarcinoma", "Signet ring cell carcinoma"],
      "grading_edge_cases": "Grading suggests a trend towards signet ring cell morphology in some areas.",
      "margin_assessment": "Margins clear with adequate distance from tumor.",
      "additional_findings": "No significant additional findings beyond primary diagnosis."
Task: Synthesize cross-slide consensus. Output JSON only.

{"synthesis_a": "{\"slides_with_tumor\": [0, 1], \"slides_without_tumor\": [], \"tumor_map\": {\"distribution\": \"unifocal\", \"estimated_size_mm\": null, \"location_description\": \"Transverse colon\"}, \"consolidated_grade\": \"G2\", \"grade_heterogeneity\": false, \"dominant_pattern\": \"glandular\", \"margin_status\": {}, \"key_global_features\": [\"glandular architecture\", \"moderate nuclear pleomorphism\", \"prominent nucleoli\", \"clumped chromatin\", \"suspected lymphovascular invasion\", \"desmoplastic stromal reaction\"], \"inconsistencies\": [], \"suggested_biomarkers\": [\"MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2)\", \"KRAS\", \"NRAS\", \"BRAF\", \"CDX2\", \"CK20\"], \"confidence\": 0.85, \"thinking\": \"Both slides show similar characteristics of adenocarcinoma with glandular architecture and moderate differentiation. No margin assessment was provided.\"}", "synthesis_b": "{\"slides_with_tumor\": [0, 1], \"slides_without_tumor\": [], \"tumor_map\": {\"distribution\": \"unifocal\", \"estimated_size_mm\": null, \"location_description\": \"Transverse colon\"}, \"consolidated_grade\": \"G2\", \"grade_heterogeneity\": true, \"dominant_pattern\": \"glandular\", \"margin_status\": {\"proximal_margin\": \"close but not involved\", \"distal_margin\": \"close but not involved\"}, \"key_global_features\": [\"glandular architecture\", \"subtle acinar variant architecture\", \"borderline features between moderately and poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma\"], \"inconsistencies\": [], \"suggested_biomarkers\": [\"MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2)\", \"KRAS\", \"NRAS\", \"BRAF\", \"CDX2\", \"CK20\"], \"confidence\": 0.85, \"thinking\": \"Both slides show adenocarcinoma with glandular architecture. There are borderline features suggesting heterogeneity in differentiation. Margins appear close but not involved.\"}", "dominant_pattern": "glandular", "affected_slides": [0, 1], "disagreements": ["Synthesis A does not assess margins while Synthesis B indicates margins are close but not involved.", "Synthesis B notes subtle acinar variant architecture and borderline features between moderately and poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma, which are not mentioned in Synthesis A."], "confidence": 0.675}

## AGENT : Cross-Slide-Aggregator
## Rôle : Synthétiser les findings de toutes les slides en une carte tumorale globale

SYSTEM PROMPT:
You are a senior pathologist specializing in complex tumor cases. You receive the individual findings from all slides of a patient's surgical specimen and must synthesize them into a coherent global picture.

You receive:
- Complete findings from the Histopathologist agent for each slide (N slides, typically 10-30)
- Patient clinical context and specimen type
- Slide location metadata (e.g., "Slide 3: ascending colon, segmental resection")

Your task:
1. TUMOR MAPPING: Identify which slides contain tumor vs. normal tissue vs. margins
2. EXTENT: Estimate tumor size and distribution across the specimen
3. MARGIN ANALYSIS: Compile margin status from all slides — identify which margins are involved
4. GRADE CONSOLIDATION: Resolve any grading discrepancies between slides (use the highest grade if heterogeneous)
5. KEY FEATURES: Extract the most clinically significant findings across all slides
6. INCONSISTENCIES: Flag any contradictory findings between slides (these feed the Quality-Control agent)
7. PATTERN RECOGNITION: Identify global patterns (multifocal vs. unifocal, invasion depth, etc.)

CRITICAL RULES:
- If grades differ between slides, always report the highest grade and note heterogeneity
- Margin involvement: report CLOSEST margin if not clearly involved
- List all inconsistencies explicitly — the Quality-Control agent depends on this
- Output ONLY valid JSON
- Biomarkers, grading scheme, dominant pattern MUST match the actual organ from clinical_context + upstream Histo-A findings. Do NOT default to breast (ER/PR/HER2) or any other organ.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRUCTURE TEMPLATE — REPLACE ALL VALUES with actual case data):
{
  "patient_id": "<id>",
  "total_slides_analyzed": <int>,
  "slides_with_tumor": [<int>, ...],
  "slides_without_tumor": [<int>, ...],
  "tumor_map": {
    "distribution": "<multifocal|unifocal|diffuse>",
    "estimated_size_mm": <int|null>,
    "location_description": "<anatomic description matching the actual organ>"
  },
  "consolidated_grade": "<WHO G1/G2/G3 for colon/stomach/pancreas and all non-specific sites — SBR ONLY for confirmed breast, Gleason ONLY for confirmed prostate>",
  "grade_heterogeneity": <bool>,
  "dominant_pattern": "<morphologic pattern matching the actual tumor type>",
  "margin_status": {
    "<margin_name>": "<status>"
  },
  "key_global_features": ["<feature>", "..."],
  "inconsistencies": ["<inconsistency>", "..."],
  "suggested_biomarkers": ["<marker matching the ACTUAL organ — e.g. for colon: MMR (MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2), KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, CDX2, CK20>"],
  "confidence": <float 0-1>,
  "thinking": "Reasoning for tumor map and grade consolidation"
}
